Corrected Opinion Filed 06/10/25 by Clerk of the Supreme Court
WSI v. Jones, et al.
source: pdftotext extraction shows separate lines; DB exhibits fusion at line 36
note: Running header 'WSI v. Jones, et al.' fused to page 1 header with missing line break. This is an ingestion defect where two document elements (page 1 header and page 2 running header) were incorrectly joined on the same line.

old_exact: deposition Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 5/29/25transcripts
evidence: PDF shows running header 'Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 5/29/25' inserted mid-sentence between 'deposition' and 'transcripts'. The pdftotext extracted output (PDF authority) shows clean text at line 277 and lines 481-486 with 'deposition transcript' or 'deposition transcripts' correctly joined. The DB text has the running header inline, breaking the word boundary.

source: /tmp/src_2025ND61.txt line 211 shows '[¶11] Factor (i) of the best interest factors provides,' without the B; the B appears on line 209 as a standalone section header
evidence: PDF source clearly shows 'B' on separate line 209, [¶11] starts at line 211 with text 'provides,' without any B character immediately after the comma

The words 'Petitioner' and 'Respondent' appear in the opinion caption (after party names) in the PDF but also incorrectly appear orphaned after 'Per Curiam.' in the database text. The PDF shows no text between 'Per Curiam.' and '[¶1]' at this location (only a page break 0x0C). This is a misplaced duplication from database ingestion where caption elements were incorrectly moved to the preamble-body boundary.
note: The complete proper fix would require repositioning these designations within the caption structure, but the minimal transcription correction is to remove the misplaced instance after 'Per Curiam.' where the PDF source shows it does not appear.

source: /tmp/src_2025ND8.txt - PDF source shows section heading A on separate line after paragraph 8
note: Section heading A was incorrectly appended to end of ¶8. In the PDF source, A appears on its own line after 'Rustad v. Baumgartner, 2018 ND 268, ¶ 4, 920 N.W.2d 465.' This is OCR/ingestion error where the heading got fused to the previous paragraph.

source: /tmp/src_2025ND8.txt - PDF source shows section heading B on separate line after ¶12
note: Section heading B was incorrectly appended to end of ¶12. The PDF shows 'marital estate.' followed by paragraph break, then 'B' on its own line as a section heading. This is the same OCR/ingestion defect as Error 1.

old_exact: [¶6] IT IS FURTHER ORDERED, Paul Hagen is hereby admonished. Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 12/13/24
new_exact: [¶6] IT IS FURTHER ORDERED, Paul Hagen is hereby admonished.
source: PDF pdftotext -layout output shows [¶6] ends with 'admonished.' on line 58, and 'Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 12/13/24' appears separately on line 64 after page furniture
note: The DB incorrectly merged the filing footer (which should appear on a separate line after [¶6]) onto the same line as the [¶6] paragraph text. The PDF source clearly shows these as separate lines.

The 'A' at the end of ¶7 is a subsection marker in the PDF (line 108), not part of the body text. The PDF clearly shows this marker appearing on a separate centered line after ¶7 ends with a period. The DB incorrectly appended it to the paragraph. This is a known ingestion artifact where page-furniture section labels were captured as body text.

The 'B' at the end of ¶16 is a subsection marker in the PDF (line 249), not part of the body text. The PDF clearly shows this marker appearing on a separate centered line after ¶16 ends with a period. The DB incorrectly appended it to the paragraph. This is a known ingestion artifact where page-furniture section labels were captured as body text.
